concepts.name: Operational Reality
concepts.definition: An event that can be reconstructed and verified through the sequence Decision, Cost, Trace, and Time.

concepts.name: Decision
concepts.definition: The selection of one possibility among multiple alternatives by an agent.

concepts.name: Cost
concepts.definition: The resource expenditure, risk, sacrifice, or irreversible change generated by a decision.

concepts.name: Trace
concepts.definition: The observable effect or record that allows reconstruction of an event.

concepts.name: Time
concepts.definition: The temporal exposure through which an event can be verified, persisted, or decay.

concepts.name: Agent
concepts.definition: The human, institutional, or artificial entity responsible for initiating a decision.

concepts.name: Operational Event
concepts.definition: An event that satisfies the full sequence Decision, Cost, Trace, and Time.

concepts.name: Narrative Event
concepts.definition: An event description that lacks one or more elements required for operational verification.
